chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ لِلْكَفَنِ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – What is recommended from the clothes for the shroud and what is disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/319)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/319)
text: يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ يَكُونَ فِي كَفَنِهِ ثَوْبٌ كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ نَظِيفٌ فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ يُكَفَّنَ فِيمَا كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira, from one of his companions who said,
‘It is recommended that there happens to be in his shroud a (piece of cloth) which he used to pray Salaat in, clean, for that is recommended that you enshroud him in what he used to pray Salaat in’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is desirable to keep for shroud a piece which one has used in Salah (prayer). It should be clean; it is desirable.”’
